# RustWerk

> Git-native, AI-agent-friendly project orchestration
> CLI. Single binary, zero config, JSON-native.

RustWerk manages tasks, dependencies, effort tracking,
and schedule visualization from the command line. All
project state lives in `.rustwerk/project.json` —
committed alongside your code.

## Quickstart

```
rustwerk init "My Project"
rustwerk task add "Task title" --id TASK-ID \
  --complexity 5 --effort 2D
rustwerk task list --available
rustwerk task status TASK-ID in-progress
rustwerk task status TASK-ID done
```

## Core Concepts

- **Tasks** have an ID, title, status, dependencies,
  complexity, effort estimate, assignee, and tags
- **Dependencies** form a DAG (cycles rejected). Task B
  depends on A means A must finish before B starts
- **Status workflow**: todo -> in-progress -> done (also
  blocked, on-hold). Use `--force` to bypass
- **Critical path**: longest chain of remaining work,
  marked with `*` in listings
- **Batch commands**: atomic JSON operations via stdin
  or file — all succeed or none are applied

## Task Management

```
rustwerk task add "Title" [--id ID] [--desc DESC] \
  [--complexity N] [--effort AMT] [--tags TAGS]
rustwerk task update <ID> [--title T] [--desc D] \
  [--tags T]
rustwerk task remove <ID>
rustwerk task status <ID> <STATUS> [--force]
rustwerk task list [--available] [--active] \
  [--status S] [--assignee DEV] [--chain ID] \
  [--tag TAG]
rustwerk task assign <ID> [DEV_ID]
rustwerk task unassign <ID>
rustwerk task depend <FROM> <TO>
rustwerk task undepend <FROM> <TO>
rustwerk task describe <ID>
```

## Effort Tracking

```
rustwerk effort estimate <ID> <AMOUNT>
rustwerk effort log <ID> <AMOUNT> [--dev DEV] \
  [--note NOTE]
```

Units: H (hours), D (days=8H), W (weeks=40H),
M (months=160H). Fractional values supported.
Task must be in-progress to log effort.

## Developer Management

```
rustwerk dev add <ID> <NAME> [--email E] [--role R]
rustwerk dev remove <ID>
rustwerk dev list
```

## Visualization and Reports

```
rustwerk show                  # project summary
rustwerk status                # compact dashboard
rustwerk tree [--remaining]    # dependency tree
rustwerk gantt [--remaining]   # ASCII Gantt chart
rustwerk report complete       # completion summary
rustwerk report effort         # effort by developer
rustwerk report bottlenecks    # blocking tasks
```

## Batch Commands (Atomic JSON)

```
echo '[
  {"command": "task.add", "args": {
    "title": "A", "id": "A", "complexity": 3
  }},
  {"command": "task.add", "args": {
    "title": "B", "id": "B"
  }},
  {"command": "task.depend", "args": {
    "from": "B", "to": "A"
  }}
]' | rustwerk batch
```

Available commands: task.add, task.remove, task.update,
task.status, task.assign, task.unassign, task.depend,
task.undepend, effort.log, effort.estimate, dev.add,
dev.remove.

Limits: 10 MB input, 1000 commands per batch.

## Task ID Conventions

IDs are uppercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores.
Use mnemonic prefixes by area (e.g. DOM-, CLI-, API-).
Auto-generated if omitted: T0001, T0002, etc.

## Status Values

todo, in-progress, blocked, done, on-hold.

Valid transitions:
- todo -> in-progress, on-hold
- in-progress -> done, blocked, on-hold
- blocked -> in-progress, todo
- on-hold -> todo, in-progress

## Environment Variables

- `RUSTWERK_USER`: default developer ID for assign
  and effort log commands

## Project Detection

RustWerk looks for `.rustwerk/` starting from the
current directory and walking up, similar to git.

## Optional: docs

- [User Manual](docs/manual.md): complete reference
  with examples
- [Project File Spec](docs/project-file-spec.md):
  JSON schema for .rustwerk/project.json
